pattern: Greedily flipping the most negative numbers first, then spending any leftover flips on the smallest magnitude value, maximizes the resulting sum.
hints: Flipping a negative number always helps, and you should attack the most negative ones first.
After the negatives are gone, remaining flips on a positive number only hurt, so pour all leftover flips into the single smallest magnitude entry.
Sort ascending and negate negatives while flips remain; if flips are left over and odd, subtract twice the smallest absolute value, otherwise the sum is unchanged.

edge_inputs.description: one flip turns the smallest positive negative
leftover flips land on a zero so sum is unaffected
more flips than negatives, even leftover keeps sum
leftover odd flips waste on smallest magnitude positive
all positives with one mandatory flip

stress.description: random mix of negatives positives and zeros with varied k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 5000))
    nums = [rng.randint(-100, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, 200)
    return [nums, k]
stress.note: k can exceed the count of negatives to exercise leftover handling
